---
title: "Introduction to Movement Metrics"
author: "Wenjing Xu 徐雯靓 (wenjing.xu@senckenberg.de)"
date: '`r format(Sys.time(), "%d %B %Y")`'
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 4
    toc_float: 
      collapsed: true
      smooth_scroll: true
    number_sections: false
    #theme: united
    #highlight: tango
pdf_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE) # display both code and results 
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.pos = 'H')
```

--------------------------------------------------------

# Introduction
Linear infrastructure ... 



In this exercise, we will cover:

1. xx
    + xx

## Load Libraries and data
Load the required libraries and remove everything held in [R's](https://cran.r-project.org/) memory.


`BaBA` is not hosted on CRAN and needs to be installed directly from GitHub: 
```{r install and load BaBA}
require("devtools")
devtools::install_github("wx-ecology/BaBA") ## <<--- may need to install locally
```


```{r packages, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, results='hide'}
# Remove from memory
rm(list=ls())

library(tidyverse, quietly = TRUE) 
library(leaflet,quietly = TRUE)
library(RColorBrewer)  # for color palettes
library(sf)
library(mapview) # for quick spatial data viz
library(gridExtra, quietly = TRUE)

```

# Types of Barrier Behaviors 

<div style="float:right">
![Barrier behaviour categories classified by Barrier Behaviour Analysis.](./Figures/BABA.jpg)

[[[[EXPLAIN DIFFERENT PARAMETERS]]]]


# pronghorn example 
```{r read pronghorn and fence data}
data("pronghorn")
data("fences")
```


```{r data check}
st_crs(pronghorn) == st_crs(fences)
```

For simplicity, we visualize two individual tracks overlapping with fence to make sure the two data overlap. This time we use `mapview` package, which is great for quick spatial data visualization for its simple syntax. 
```{r data visualization 1 }
  mapView(pronghorn, zcol = "Animal.ID", layer.name = "Pronghorn pts", cex = 1) +
  mapView(fences, color = "grey", layer.name = "Fencelines") 
```

```{r data visualization 1 }

pronghorn.lines <- pronghorn %>% 
  mt_as_move2(., time_column = "date", track_id_column = "Animal.ID") %>%
  mt_track_lines() 


  mapView(pronghorn.lines, zcol = "Animal.ID",layer.name = "Pronghorn lines", cex = 1) +
    mapView(fences, color = "grey", layer.name = "Fencelines") 
```

```{r run baba 1, eval = FALSE }

# when choosing exporting images, it will take a while to run 
results_prong <- BaBA(animal = pronghorn, 
                      barrier = fences, 
                      b_time = 4, p_time = 36, d = 110, max_cross = 4,
                      export_images = T, 
                      img_path = "./Output/BaBA_viz_pronghorn/", img_suffix = "pronghorn")

# you can read results here:
# results_prong <- read_rds("./2_ClusteringAndSegmentation/Output/BaBA_results_prong.rds")
```
```{r view baba result 1 }
# View BaBA results

# one output is classification table that summarizes .... 
head(results_prong$classification)

# plot encounter event locations

  mapView(results_prong$encounters, zcol = "eventTYPE",
          col.regions = brewer.pal(n = 5, name = "Set1"), layer.name = "Pronghorn BaBA", cex = 4) +
    mapView(fences, color = "grey", layer.name = "Fencelines") 

```

[[[[ walk thought results ]]]]



# Wildebeest example 

```{r read WB data}
# load data and filter out the unreliable locations
WB <- read_rds("./Data/wildebeest_3hr_adehabitat.rds") %>%
  as_tibble() %>% 
  rename(Animal.ID = animal_id) %>%
  mutate(Animal.ID = factor(Animal.ID)) %>%
  drop_na(x, y) %>%
  st_as_sf(., coords = c("x", "y"), crs = st_crs(32737))

# read fence polylins
fence.kenya <-  st_read("./Data/Fencelines2010_UTM37S.gpkg") 

```



BaBA requires data in projected coordinate system, same for linear infrastructure and animal data.
```{r data transform}
st_crs(WB) == st_crs(fence.kenya)

```

# visualize movement and fences


```{r data viz}

WB.lines <- WB %>% 
  mt_as_move2(., time_column = "date", track_id_column = "Animal.ID") %>%
  mt_track_lines() 

mapView(WB.lines, zcol = "Animal.ID",
          layer.name = "Wildebeest", cex = 1) +
    mapView(fence.kenya, color = "grey", layer.name = "Fencelines") 
```
We can see the fences are very different from the wyoming fences. so the parameters need to be different to fit context. 

Run one individual with data output first. 
First define b_time and p_time parameters based on data resolution and ecological contexts. Then, testing a series of buffer distance to find the optimal one.


```{r filter to one ind}

WB.sf.1ind = WB %>% 
  filter(Animal.ID == unique(Animal.ID)[1])
```


```{r run baba 3, eval = FALSE}
results_WB1 <- BaBA(animal = WB.sf.1ind, 
                      barrier = fence.kenya, 
                      d = 100,
                      interval = 3, units = "hours",
                      b_time = 3, p_time = 36,  max_cross = 0,
                      export_images = T, 
                      img_path = "./Output/BaBA_viz_wb/b3_d100/", img_suffix = "wb_b3_d100")

# directly read results here
# results_WB1 <- read_rds("./2_ClusteringAndSegmentation/Output/BaBA_results_WB1.rds")

results_WB2 <- BaBA(animal = WB.sf.1ind, 
                      barrier = fence.kenya, 
                      d = 50,
                      interval = 3, units = "hours",
                      b_time = 3, p_time = 36,  max_cross = 0,
                      export_images = T, 
                      img_path = "./Output/BaBA_viz_wb/b3_d50/", img_suffix = "wb_b3_d50")


# directly read results here
# results_WB2 <- read_rds("./2_ClusteringAndSegmentation/Output/BaBA_results_WB2.rds")

results_WB3 <- BaBA(animal = WB.sf.1ind, 
                      barrier = fence.kenya, 
                      d = 150,
                      interval = 3, units = "hours",
                      b_time = 3, p_time = 36,  max_cross = 0,
                      export_images = T, 
                      img_path = "./Output/BaBA_viz_wb/b3_d150/", img_suffix = "wb_b3_d150")

# directly read results here
# results_WB3 <- read_rds("./2_ClusteringAndSegmentation/Output/BaBA_results_WB3.rds")
```
most quick cross are likely due to gps or fence location error. they are all really just bounce. 



now testing different buffer distance. 

```{r run baba 5, eval = FALSE}
event = tibble()
for (dist in seq(10,200, by = 10)) {
results_WB_dist <- BaBA(animal = WB.sf.1ind, 
                      barrier = fence.kenya, 
                      d = dist,
                      interval = 3, units = "hours",
                      b_time = 3, p_time = 36,  max_cross = 0,
                      export_images = F)
event.dist <- tibble(results_WB_dist$classification) %>% mutate(buffer_dist = dist)
event <- rbind(event, event.dist)
}

# directly read results here
# event <- read_rds("./2_ClusteringAndSegmentation/Output/BaBA_results_WB_dist.rds")

```

```{r compare dist results}

event_summary <- event %>%
  group_by(buffer_dist, eventTYPE) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(buffer_dist) %>%
  mutate(proportion = count / sum(count)) 


p_all_type <- event_summary %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(buffer_dist), y = proportion, fill= eventTYPE)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(x = "Buffer Distance", y = "Event Count", fill = "Event Type") +
  theme_minimal()

p_all_type_line <- event_summary %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(buffer_dist), y = proportion, group= eventTYPE, color = eventTYPE)) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(x = "Buffer Distance", y = "Event Count", fill = "Event Type") +
  theme_minimal()
grid.arrange(p_all_type, p_all_type_line)
```

Looks like Bounce is the only one that levels off - which makes sense. look at bounce: 
```{r compare dist results2}
p_all_type2_line <- event_summary %>%
  filter(eventTYPE %in% c("Bounce", "Quick_Cross")) %>%
  mutate(eventTYPE2 = "short_event" ) %>% 
  group_by(buffer_dist) %>%
  summarise(proportion = sum(proportion)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = buffer_dist, y = proportion)) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(x = "Buffer Distance", y = "Event Count", fill = "Event Type") +
  theme_minimal()
p_all_type2_line 

```



</div>
|           <span style="color:tomato">**QUESTION 1: ** What can be some ecologically meaning ways to determine buffer distances? </span>
<br/><br/>

---------------------
Further readings:

+ Xu W, Dejid N, Herrmann V, Sawyer H, Middleton AD. (2021). Barrier Behaviour Analysis (BaBA) reveals extensive effects of fencing on wide-ranging ungulates. Journal of Applied Ecology. 58(4), 690-698. https://doi.org/10.1111/1365-2664.13806

+ Aikens, E. O., Merkle, J. A., Xu, W., & Sawyer, H. (2025). Pronghorn movements and mortality during extreme weather highlight the critical importance of connectivity. Current Biology.https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cub.2025.03.010 



 <center> -----------------     (ﾉ◕ヮ◕)ﾉ*:･ﾟ✧  THE END :･ﾟ･ﾟ     ------------------ </center>
